112.reason: [After detecting an invalid single '&' or '|', the lexer should not continue normal tokenization; however, it fails to push back the second character (which may be part of a valid token) and does not break or skip properly, potentially causing misalignment in parsing. More critically, after setting flag=0, the program still continues processing tokens instead of stopping lexical analysis immediately as required by the problem statement which says each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error and should output only the error. Additionally, the current logic does not push back the read character when encountering a single '&', leading to loss of that character in subsequent parsing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [After detecting an invalid single '&' or '|', the lexer should not continue normal tokenization; however, it fails to push back the second character (which may be part of a valid token) and does not break or skip properly, potentially causing misalignment in parsing. More critically, after setting flag=0, the program still continues processing tokens instead of stopping lexical analysis immediately as required by the problem statement which says each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error and should output only the error. Additionally, the current logic does not push back the read character when encountering a single '&', leading to loss of that character in subsequent parsing.]
